channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Yelp has quietly become a home services powerhouse
item.description: When the COVID-19 pandemic abruptly shut down normal life more than four years ago, few tech companies endured more wrenching change than Yelp.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Dirty romance novels are shedding their stigma thanks to TikTok
item.description: By now everyone is familiar with #BookTok—but have you stumbled across its spicier cousin #SmutTok? “Smut” refers to books that include one or more sexually explicit scenes and surprisingly (or perhaps unsurprisingly) the erotic category has found a growing fan base on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Blue screens, begone: Microsoft puts an end to troubles caused by optional Windows 11 update, and you don't even have to do anything to fix your PC
item.description: Microsoft's Known Issue Rollback (KIR) automatically resolves problems caused by Windows 11 update KB5043145, seamlessly fixing crashes and other issues.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: California governor vetoes controversial AI safety bill
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom on Sunday vetoed a hotly contested artificial intelligence safety bill after the tech industry raised objections, saying it could drive AI companies from the state and hinder innovation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat has failed to act on rampant reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5 million by federal safety regulators for failing to report robotaxi crash details
item.description: The autonomous vehicle company Cruise has been ordered to pay a $1.5 million fine for failing to properly report post-crash details involving a woman who last year was dragged 20 feet in San Francisco by one of its self-driving cars.
item.dc:creator: Kristin J. Bender, The Mercury News

item.title: AppliedXL and Associated Press team up to use AI to sift through federal regulations
item.description: In an era when local newsrooms are stretched thinner than ever, keeping up with the constant deluge of government regulations and updates can feel impossible.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: Drone delivery service Wing teams with Serve to expand delivery in Dallas
item.description: Serve Robotics, which makes autonomous sidewalk delivery bots, and Wing, Alphabet’s on-demand drone delivery service, announced a pilot partnership on Tuesday meant to expand autonomous last-mile food delivery in Dallas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Donald Trump’s crypto venture begins sign-ups while business purpose remains unclear
item.description: World Liberty is just the latest of Trump’s crypto efforts. The Republican presidential nominee has made supporting crypto a part of his platform as he seeks reelection in November.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: JNUC 2024: Jamf brings AI and more to Apple enterprise
item.description: Apple management and security services provider Jamf unveiled a series of tools and features to support Apple admins at its well-attended JNUC event Oct. 1, with a focus on AI, security, and all-new solutions to make it easier to manage large Apple fleets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Does your Halloween rager really need a Partiful invite?
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Scammers’ sneaky new tactic preying on people who’ve lost their iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to protect yourself with eight steps if your iPhone is lost or stolen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAA confirms SpaceX Falcon 9 is grounded because of Crew-9 launch issue
item.description: The agency is investigating why the rocket's second stage missed its deorbit landing target zone after this weekend's Crew-9 launch.
item.dc:creator: Richard Tribou, The Mercury News

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: Australia’s government says a code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Legacy of Legacy Code: A Reflection on Code Maintenance
item.description: The importance of code maintenance and the legacy of legacy code in software development
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fortnite maker accuses Samsung, Google of colluding to block app store competitors
item.description: Fortnite maker Epic Games filed a lawsuit Monday accusing Google and Samsung, the world’s largest Android maker, of conspiring to block third-party competitors of the Google Play Store.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: DirecTV, Dish to merge to create largest US pay-TV provider
item.description: Under the terms of the transaction, DirecTV will acquire Dish TV and Sling TV from EchoStar Corp. for a nominal consideration of $1 plus the assumption of about $9.75 billion of debt.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Kobiton preps AI-enabled testing tools for mobile apps
item.description: Mobile apps tester Kobiton announced plans to roll out an AI issue aggregation engine early next year. The AI-driven engine will allow users to group related errors and identify shared commonalities across test sessions, the company said. Kobiton made the announcement September 26.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kristen Bell is a Meta AI voice after criticizing company in post
item.description: After posting an "opt out" declaration on Instagram, the actress has now signed with Meta to use her voice in its AI chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: X releases its first transparency report since Elon Musk's takeover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Social media platform X on Wednesday published its first transparency report since the company was purchased by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rise of influencer skincare routines causing harm to young girls
item.description: "It is, however, a little frustrating as a professional in the industry to see kids overutilizing products that aren't necessary for their skin," said Old City Med Spa aesthetician Mahala Brien.
item.dc:creator: Lori Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: CrowdStrike executive apologizes to Congress for July global tech outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An executive at cybersecurity company CrowdStrike apologized in testimony to Congress for sparking a global technology outage over the summer. “We let our customers down,” said Adam Meyers, who leads CrowdStrike's threat intelligence division, in a hearing before a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Age of AI 'Superintelligence'
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: F5, NetApp team to streamline AI app deployments
item.description: F5 and NetApp announced plans to combine their technologies to help enterprise customers securely deploy AI applications across multicloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Video game actors' union calls for strike against 'League of Legends'
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — “League of Legends” is caught in the middle of a dispute between Hollywood’s actors union and an audio company that provides voiceover services for the blockbuster online multiplayer game.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top 4 reasons you might still need VPN with cellular service
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains why you may still need a virtual private network even if you're using your phone's cellular network.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
